title: Com[oe]dia Juris
content:
Est omne jus forense quasi com[oe]dia;
        Hic advocatus maximas partes agit
        Laudatus undique a procuratoribus,
        Labore vocis redditus ditissimus;
        Cui brevia nil forensis et quaestus valent
        Silenter ille spectat, at pro pr�mio
        Fruitur quietus optime com[oe]dia.
meaning:
This evocative piece by James Williams, titled "Com[oe]dia Juris", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
